skill.name: next-js-better-auth-integration
skill.description: Guides implementation of Better Auth in Next.js App Router projects, including sessions, providers, protected routes, and security checks.
skill.summary: Plan and implement Better Auth authentication patterns for Next.js App Router applications.
skill.author: Claude

security_audit.summary: Reviewed the three static findings in SKILL.md and found no confirmed malicious or unsafe behavior. The flagged lines are descriptive authentication guidance, not executable code, credential access, weak cryptography, or network reconnaissance. No prompt injection attempt or data exfiltration intent was found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Secure Cookie Guidance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged browser credential files at SKILL.md:142. The line recommends secure, HTTP-only cookies for sessions, which is defensive authentication guidance and does not access browser credential files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line contains a secure cookie recommendation only. No filesystem path, browser profile access, or credential extraction behavior appears in the skill.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptographic Algorithm
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged weak cryptography at SKILL.md:7. The line is frontmatter description text for a conceptual Better Auth integration skill and does not name or configure a cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is plain metadata text. There is no MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak algorithm configuration at that location.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Network Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The static analyzer flagged network reconnaissance at SKILL.md:122. The line advises using server components for server-side session access and does not describe port scanning, probing, or network enumeration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding context is App Router integration guidance. No command, endpoint list, scanner, or reconnaissance workflow is present.

content.value_statement: Authentication in App Router projects often spans server components, middleware, sessions, and client state. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code a focused checklist for implementing Better Auth patterns.
actual_capabilities: Outlines Better Auth initialization steps for Next.js App Router projects.
Guides session access across server components and client components.
Describes protected route patterns using middleware and redirects.
Lists JWT, cookie, CSRF, rate limiting, and secret management considerations.
Frames expected inputs and outputs for authentication implementation work.
limitations: Provides conceptual guidance, not a complete generated application.
Does not include framework-specific code snippets for every Better Auth version.
Assumes the project already uses the Next.js App Router.
Requires human review for provider settings, secrets, and production security policy.

use_cases.title: Add Authentication to a New App
use_cases.description: Plan Better Auth setup, session context, and protected pages before writing implementation code.

use_cases.title: Review Auth Architecture
use_cases.description: Check whether session handling, redirects, token behavior, and cookie settings are covered.

use_cases.title: Prepare Implementation Tasks
use_cases.description: Convert authentication requirements into focused work items for App Router, middleware, and UI states.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Basic Setup
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to outline the Better Auth setup steps for my Next.js App Router project. Include required files, session flow, and environment variables.

prompt_templates.title: Design Protected Routes
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to design protected route behavior for these pages: dashboard, settings, and billing. Include unauthenticated redirects and session checks.

prompt_templates.title: Review Session Security
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to review my planned Better Auth session strategy. Focus on cookies, JWT expiration, CSRF protection, rate limiting, and secret handling.

prompt_templates.title: Create an Auth Migration Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to create a phased migration plan from custom auth to Better Auth in a Next.js App Router app with social login and protected server components.

output_examples.input: Plan Better Auth integration for a new App Router project.
output: Recommended setup sequence for auth configuration, provider setup, and session context.
Route protection plan for public and private pages.
Security checklist covering cookies, CSRF, secrets, and rate limiting.
Assessment of server-side and client-side session checks.
Gaps to verify before production release.

best_practices: Start with session and provider requirements before changing routes.
Keep authentication secrets in managed environment variables.
Test expired sessions, invalid tokens, and unauthenticated access paths.
anti_patterns: Do not store session tokens in insecure client-side storage.
Do not protect pages only with client-side checks.
Do not ship authentication flows without rate limiting and CSRF review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a full Better Auth implementation?
faq.answer: No. It provides structured guidance for planning and reviewing the implementation.

faq.question: Is this intended for the Next.js Pages Router?
faq.answer: No. The skill is focused on Next.js App Router projects.

faq.question: Can it help with protected routes?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers middleware, server-side checks, redirects, and session state handling.

faq.question: Does it cover social login?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes provider configuration as part of the authentication planning flow.

faq.question: Does it replace a production security review?
faq.answer: No. Production settings, provider configuration, and secrets still need human review.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: It is marked for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
